text:
There once was a clever cat named Whiskers. Whiskers lived in a cozy little cottage next door. One day, while lazily snoozing on the windowsill, Whiskers heard a tapping.

Tapping... tapping!

Whiskers rubbed his eyes. That must be a bird. But it kept tapping.

Tap... tap.

Later, Whiskers heard laughter. Snicker-snicker?

Tap... tap.

It couldn't be an owl—it was nighttime. Maybe a squirrel?

Tap... tap!

Whiskers cautiously peeked out. There stood a wise old owl. It winked.

"Wrinkle-eye?"

"Yes!" exclaimed Whiskers.

"The secret!"

"Oh?"

"Yeah! You see, I love acorns!"

"Aww?"

"Yes. Every ounce."

"You know."

"Yes?"

"I've saved dozens."

"Ack!"


You think?


Why?

"I save them from trouble."

"Oh?"

"Why? You wouldn't!"

"Would you?"

"It's a
